Introduction xi
Author: Varahamihira (505 A,D.) was a scientist of rare merits,
who in spite of his regard for ancient traditions and Sages, followed
always the scientific method. His scientific thinking is illustrated by
what he says in connection with the commencement of the solstices
in III 1-3 as well as with Rahu and eclipse.1 In view of his
extraordinary and masterly treatment of the subjects of astronomy
and astrology in all their ramifications, he was held in high esteem
by scholars of ancient and medieval India, About his greatness
Al-Birunl, the Arab scholar and traveller says, "Varahamihira has
already revealed himself to us as a man who accurately knows the
shape of the world . . , On the whole his foot stands firmly on the
basis of the truth, and he clearly speaks out the truth."2 He was
